elements: row of mailbox-shaped 2D objects at varying depths
flat horizontal arrows illustrating motion direction and speed

interactions.label: Camera Speed
interactions.teaches: Adjusting the slider simulates the camera's lateral motion; higher positive values increase the speed to the right and negative values to the left.

demo_steps.title: Stationary Camera
demo_steps.narration: The camera is stationary with camera speed set to zero. There is no observed motion in the scene right now.

demo_steps.title: Camera Moves Right
demo_steps.narration: Increasing the camera speed to the right makes nearby mailbox shapes appear to move leftward quickly, while distant ones barely shift.

demo_steps.title: Camera Moves Left
demo_steps.narration: Reversing the slider to a negative value causes the camera to move leftward, making nearby mailboxes appear to swiftly move rightwards.

demo_steps.title: Parallax: Reduced Movement at Distance
demo_steps.narration: With an increased camera speed, notice that mailboxes at a greater distance from the camera appear nearly stationary, illustrating the parallax effect.

plan.camera_suggestion: Front-facing orthographic view centered on the row of objects, emphasizing the relative motion and depth effects.

inline_constraints.default_view: must match the original image silhouette, size, label scale, and crop
inline_constraints.visible_ui: at most 2 compact sliders/toggles, visible near an edge with no filled panel, and must not cover important geometry
inline_constraints.explainers: hover tooltips and click popups only
inline_constraints.framing: match the source crop and whitespace; do not force-fill the iframe if the original has margins
inline_constraints.camera: infer the source camera angle/projection/zoom so the first rendered frame is a drop-in visual replacement

plan.notes: Ensure the arrows remain flat and consistent in style with the original; use an orthographic camera for coherent object representation as the camera slides laterally. Mailbox shapes replace cube geometries for a more accurate visual analogy.

elements: series of objects moving past the camera
horizontal arrows showing direction and speed of motion
background grid or scene for depth reference

interactions.label: Camera Speed
interactions.teaches: Increasing slider value represents faster lateral motion of the camera; negative values indicate opposite direction.

demo_steps.title: Initial Setup: No Motion
demo_steps.narration: Initially, the camera is stationary and no lateral motion is observed in the scene.

demo_steps.title: Slow Lateral Motion
demo_steps.narration: As the slider moves to a positive value, the camera begins to translate to the right. Nearby objects appear to move quickly to the left while distant objects barely shift.

demo_steps.title: Reverse Motion
demo_steps.narration: Adjusting the slider to a negative value, the camera moves to the left. This means close objects now appear to move rapidly rightwards.

demo_steps.title: Zero Visibility at Infinitive Depth
demo_steps.narration: Objects far away almost seem stationary regardless of the slider value, simulating the parallax effect where only nearby objects exhibit notable motion.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: Side view with camera and objects aligned horizontally, emphasizing lateral translation and depth interaction.

inline_constraints.default_view: match the original image silhouette, size, label scale, and crop
inline_constraints.visible_ui: at most 2 compact sliders/toggles, visible near an edge with no filled panel, and must not cover important geometry
inline_constraints.explainers: hover tooltips and click popups only
inline_constraints.framing: match the source crop and whitespace; do not force-fill the iframe if the original has margins
inline_constraints.camera: infer the source camera angle/projection/zoom so the first rendered frame is a drop-in visual replacement

interactionPlan.notes: Use orthographic projection to keep object sizes consistent as the camera moves sideways. Animate object motion in opposite direction to camera slider manipulation.

attempts.html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: Original figure has more detailed mailbox shapes, not cubes
Generated figure uses a grid, not present in the original
Arrows in the generated figure are in 3D while original uses 2D
Generated figure is in perspective view unlike the original
Figures are inadvertently three-dimensional when they should be flat icons
evaluation.notes: The use of 3D cubes and perspective view diverges significantly from the 2D flat icons of the original.
action_items: Replace the cube geometries with 2D shapes that resemble the mailboxes in the original reference.
Remove the grid and adjust the camera to an orthographic view for a more accurate match.
Ensure arrows are flat and match the style and color of the original figure.

feedback.rationale: Wrong-Primitives and Camera-Wrong failure modes with major conceptual execution issues indicate the need to realign the plan to a consistent 2D representation instead of fixing execution details.

Ensure arrows are flat and match the style and color of the original figure.
